statement.markdown: Implement a queue that supports enqueue and dequeue operations. The queue should raise an error when trying to dequeue from an empty queue. You need to fix the dequeue method in the given code.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a list of numbers.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a number popped from the queue.
statement.notes: The queue should support at least 10000 items.

examples.explanation: Dequeue the first item from the queue.
Try to dequeue from an empty queue.

hints.text: Check the implementation of the dequeue method.
Consider using a front pointer to track the first item in the queue.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The correct approach is to use a front pointer to track the first item in the queue and a rear pointer to track the last item added.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: Use a list and pop the first item from it. However, this approach has a time complexity of O(n) for dequeue operation.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a front pointer and a rear pointer to track the queue.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a linked list to implement the queue.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution has a time complexity of O(1) for enqueue and dequeue operations.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to update the front pointer after dequeue operation
Using the rear pointer to dequeue from the queue

testCases.output: Error: trying to dequeue from an empty queue
